stemHtml: You are climbing to 9000 ft with the autopilot engaged and a 2500 ft/min rate of climb. Passing 7500 ft ATC tells you to stop climb at 8000 ft. Passing 7900 ft the pilot selects ALT HOLD mode. What is the result?

options.a: Aircraft will overshoot 8000 ft to comply with acceleration limits, slowly level off then descend back to the altitude where ALT HOLD was pressed
options.c: ALT HOLD is prevented from engaging when there’s a risk of exceeding g-load and pilot will descend to 8000 ft
options.d: Aircraft will overshoot 8000 ft as compliance with acceleration limits will be prioritized over levelling off, then pilot intervention will be required to descend.
